objective: Trace code paths for 3 flagged results to verify sample definitions and methodology per MS1-MS2 standards

sample.filter: Transitions with valid Census codes in both origin and destination

methodology.description: Conditional logit comparison of Wasserstein vs kernel distances
methodology.comparison: Both models fit on SAME sample with same random seed (42) and n_alternatives (10)

t_module_geometry.notes: Sample size documented in output JSON. Filter is minimal (valid codes only). No unexplained restrictions.

i_module_asymmetric.headline_justification: Baseline Wasserstein model on full 89,329 sample. HC4 ('Asymmetry is HETEROGENEOUS') correctly notes ratio varies by sample.

methodology.description: Asymmetric conditional logit with separate β_up and β_down coefficients

i_module_asymmetric.notes: Baseline 2.11 is full sample. Variants (1.12, 0.06) are from documented robustness checks with explicit filters. LEDGER correctly states heterogeneity.

methodology.geometry_model: Conditional logit fitted on train, applied to holdout
methodology.historical_baseline: Empirical transition probabilities from training data

s_module_shock.notes: Sample sizes explicitly documented in output. Year partitioning clearly specified. Evaluation on clean holdout.

results_verified_as_stated: I Module: Asymmetry ratio = 2.11 baseline (n=89,329), heterogeneous by sample as documented
S Module: ΔLL = +23,119 (train n=97,236, holdout n=8,880)
methodology_gaps_found: LEDGER.md inline text does not show sample sizes (but experiment outputs do document them)
M Module Demand per-origin ρ = 0.316 uses 'common destinations' methodology vs rigorous 0.128 - this remains a HIGH priority correction
recommendations: Update LEDGER.md T Module section to inline sample size (n=89,329)
Update LEDGER.md I Module section to clarify 2.11 is baseline full-sample Wasserstein
Update LEDGER.md S Module section to inline sample sizes
